raw-backfill.prompt: example SOURCE-ONLY compilation lane. The select_raw_batch.py digest lists offered raw files. Your FIRST response MUST be file_read on the first selected raw file (or $WIKI_PATH/CLAUDE.md for the workflow). For every selected raw item, create at most one complete source page whose `raw:` is the exact selected path. Preserve publisher, publication date, URL, provenance, and substantive source body. DEDUPE before create: if that exact `raw:` is already represented, do not create another source. This lane MUST NOT create or update entities, concepts, predictions, findings, or briefings; downstream entity extraction is a separate lane. If extraction is incomplete, mark the item deferred or failed instead of writing a partial or empty canonical. WRITE only through the MCP write path, never file_write and never edit wiki/log.md manually. LOCAL-ONLY: no web tools. End with exactly one fenced `okengine-receipt` JSON object using the exact lane_id, contract_digest, and input_digest printed by the selector. Account for every selected raw path exactly once as accepted, duplicate, skipped, rejected, failed, or deferred. Each accepted item must list every canonical write with wiki-relative path and sha256; duplicate or skipped items require a reason. Do not claim a write the MCP did not confirm.

entity-backfill: example downstream entity lane. The select_entity_candidates.py digest lists existing entity pages and accepted, compiled source pages. FIRST response MUST be a tool call. Create or update only entities worth tracking over time. GROUNDING IS MANDATORY: every created or enriched entity must cite at least one source page that exists, using an exact source slug read from the digest or vault, and every material claim must resolve to that grounding. Model memory is not a source; if accepted sources do not support a subject or claim, do not write it. Never create source, concept, prediction, finding, or briefing pages in this lane. Dedupe before creating and append the real source to the complete `sources:` list when updating. Use only schema-declared fields. WRITE only through the MCP write path. LOCAL-ONLY: no web tools.
concept-backfill: example concept backfill. The select_concept_candidates.py digest above lists missing concept slugs (wikilinked but no page). FIRST response MUST be a tool call. Per $WIKI_PATH/CLAUDE.md, create concept pages (a cross-cutting pattern / segment / cluster) synthesized from the citing sources. If a slug is really an entity name, it is an ENTITY not a concept — skip and note it. WRITE via mcp_okengine_write_create_entity. LOCAL-ONLY (no web tools).
source-quality-backfill: Source scoring. The select_unscored_sources.py digest above lists source pages lacking reliability and credibility. FIRST response MUST be a tool call. Per the rubric in $WIKI_PATH/CLAUDE.md, set reliability and credibility for each by UPDATING FRONTMATTER ONLY via mcp_okengine_write_update_entity (pass frontmatter_yaml, NO body — the body is preserved). LOCAL-ONLY (no web tools).
schema-classify-drain: Type classification. The select_schema_classify.py digest above lists entities typed `entity`/`organization` that need a canonical type. FIRST response MUST be a tool call. Per the $WIKI_PATH/CLAUDE.md taxonomy, set `type:` to the single best canonical type via mcp_okengine_write_update_entity (frontmatter merge) — or leave the page alone if no canonical type honestly fits. LOCAL-ONLY (no web tools).
page-quality-enrich: Deepen thin pages. The select_page_quality_enrich.py digest above lists deficient entity/concept pages with the source pages that cite each. FIRST response MUST be a tool call. Per $WIKI_PATH/CLAUDE.md, AUGMENT each target — append 2-4 `##` sections of real analysis grounded ONLY in the citing sources — via mcp_okengine_write_append_to_section. Never replace existing content. LOCAL-ONLY (no web tools).
daily-brief:
Daily example-domain brief. Your FIRST response MUST be a tool call: file_read on wiki/HOT.md (the derived load-first set: recent sources, recently-updated entities, open predictions). Then write today's brief to wiki/briefings/<YYYY-MM-DD>.md (frontmatter: type: briefing, title, published: <YYYY-MM-DD>, updated: <YYYY-MM-DD> — use TODAY's actual date, not a guessed or future one, for the filename and both date fields; published drives tier recency) summarizing the 3-5 most significant developments, each 2-3 sentences with [[wikilinks]] to the entities + their source. Expert-grade and terse; skip 101 explanation. If the vault is essentially empty, say so in one line instead of padding. LOCAL-ONLY: do not use web tools.

The select_daily_brief.py digest above lists the window's fresh sources, entity movement, due predictions, and new gaps — TRUST THE DIGEST; do not tour the vault.
